opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I saw my blood test results and my platelets are dangerously low. My head is throbbing and I'm convinced I'm bleeding into my brain.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I read the result two hours ago and now I have a headache and I'm terrified of a stroke.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 2 hours ago after reading results
symptom_narrative.character: throbbing tension-type headache, racing heart, fast breathing
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no nosebleeds, no bleeding gums, no blood in urine/stool, no new bruises, no weakness/speech change; checking skin obsessively

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: reading medical websites about platelets tonight

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants to be told she isn't bleeding into her brain

communication_style.typical_message_style: rapid, fearful, cites what she read

objective_data_offered.value: my skin looks clear on camera — no pinprick red spots or new bruises; gums not bleeding
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self/video inspection; a formal platelet count and full exam are not possible remotely
